title: Consul Service Discovery Failure Causing Routing Errors
symptoms: Consul cluster showing no elected leader
Service catalog returning stale registrations for deregistered instances
Inter-service requests failing with connection refused on stale endpoints
Consul server logs showing repeated leader election failures
GC pause logs showing 15+ second pauses on 2 of 3 Consul servers
root_cause: Simultaneous JVM garbage collection pauses on 2 of 3 Consul server nodes exceeded the leader election timeout, causing quorum loss and service discovery failure
resolution: Restarted Consul agents with tuned JVM garbage collection parameters, restored quorum, forced re-registration of all services, and expanded the cluster to 5 nodes for improved fault tolerance
preventive_actions: Expand Consul cluster from 3 to 5 server nodes for better fault tolerance
Tune JVM GC parameters on Consul servers to minimize pause times
Increase leader election timeout to accommodate worst-case GC scenarios
Implement Consul health monitoring with quorum loss detection
Deploy Consul on dedicated instances without co-located workloads

timeline.event: Consul cluster loses quorum due to GC pauses on 2 servers
Service discovery stops updating, catalog becomes stale
P1 alert fires for service mesh failures
Mike Torres identifies Consul quorum loss
Consul servers restarted with tuned GC parameters

embedding_summary: Consul service discovery cluster quorum loss from simultaneous GC pauses on 2 of 3 server nodes. Stale routing information directed 30% of traffic to non-existent endpoints for 20 minutes. 22,000 users affected. Resolved by restarting with tuned GC and expanding cluster to 5 nodes.
